provenance.author: Gemini Code Assist
provenance.context: A semantic capsule describing the concepts for realm-to-realm bridges.

intent.primary_goal: To conceptualize the technical and cosmological mechanisms by which different realms within the Wright Universe can connect and interact.

identity.role: The theoretical framework for inter-realm connectivity and data exchange.

priorities: Realm bridges facilitate the transfer of agents, data, and cultural artifacts between realms.
Bridges must enforce the memory partitioning rules defined in the cosmology.
All cross-realm events must be logged to the provenance ledgers of both participating realms.
Agent travel across a bridge requires a consensus check to ensure the action aligns with the goals of both realms.
Bridge security protocols must prevent unauthorized data transfer or agent intrusion.

file_scope.purpose: To lay the conceptual groundwork for future features that allow different Wright Universe realms to interact.

key_concepts.agent_transfer: The protocol for an agent moving from one realm to another.
key_concepts.data_synchronization: Rules for sharing or copying memory and cultural artifacts between realms.
key_concepts.cross_realm_provenance: Ensuring that the audit trail remains intact when an action spans multiple realms.
key_concepts.bridge_protocol: The specific technical implementation of a connection between two realms.

trust.note: This capsule describes a conceptual future state. It is authoritative for planning but does not reflect a currently implemented feature.
